title: Joe Mazzulla’s ‘tough love’ reminds Robert Parish of a particular Hall-of-Fame coach
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Parish compares Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla's tough love to former coach Bill Fitch.
Parish praised Mazzulla's martial arts background, explaining the coach's intensity and confident persona.
Parish highlighted Mazzulla's people skills, noting players' belief in his philosophy contributes to Celtics' success.

title: Lukas Reichel has a tall task sparking dormant part of Bruins’ lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Lukas Reichel, a 2020 first-round pick, joins Boston Bruins to invigorate the slumping third line against Winnipeg.
Reichel, acquired from the Canucks for a 2026 sixth-round pick, scored five points in three games at Providence.
Head coach Marco Sturm emphasizes Reichel's speed and playmaking to boost Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm's performance.

title: A Massachusetts police officer is running the Boston Marathon for veterans
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Laicey Ieronimo, a police officer from Carver, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Semper Fi & America’s Fund.
This will be Ieronimo's third charity run at the Boston Marathon, supporting veterans and military members.
Ieronimo is also a coach and non-profit founder, having raised over $40,000 for various causes through running.

title: Cambridge Day founder Marc Levy is back with a new Cambridge and Somerville news hub
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Marc Levy launches Cambridge Somerville Independent to cover local news in Cambridge and Somerville.
The news hub operates with a hybrid model, featuring both commercial and nonprofit elements for funding.
The Cambridge Chronicle's closure in 2022 left a coverage gap that Levy's publication aims to fill.

title: For this Boston Marathon runner, the starting line is everything
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Gregory Baxter, a 43-year-old Marine veteran, runs the Boston Marathon to honor resilience and support fellow service members.
Baxter, now a mental health professional, emphasizes the significance of the starting line over the finish line in his journey.
He supports Semper Fi & America's Fund to raise awareness and aid for service members and their families.

title: Boston City Council to weigh non-citizen voting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council considers allowing non-citizens with legal status to vote in municipal elections.
Councilor Julia Mejia's petition follows a similar stalled effort in 2023 and addresses rising anti-immigrant rhetoric.
Councilor Ed Flynn opposes, citing concerns over electoral system faith; the petition now heads to the Committee on Government Operations.

title: ‘He looks great’: Draymond Green had high praise for Jayson Tatum
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Draymond Green praised Jayson Tatum's performance after Celtics' 120-99 win over Warriors, noting Tatum's impressive return.
Tatum scored 24 points and grabbed 10 rebounds, impressing Green with his movement and ability post-injury.
Draymond Green, a former Olympic teammate, shared a light-hearted pre-game exchange with Tatum, acknowledging friendly competition.

title: Two-time cancer survivor from Easton to run Boston Marathon for Mass General
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Kevin O'Rourke, 57 from Easton, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon honoring Massachusetts General Hospital.
O'Rourke is a two-time cancer survivor, diagnosed in 2019 and 2020, running his first marathon after recovery.
He runs in memory of his father, Brian O'Rourke, who was treated for stage four cancer at Mass General in 1999.

title: Boston police officer arrested, charged with manslaughter in fatal shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston officer Nicholas O'Malley arrested for manslaughter in Stephenson King's March 11 shooting death in Roxbury.
O'Malley is set for arraignment at Boston Municipal Court Roxbury Division, according to Suffolk County DA's Office.
City councilors demand release of body camera footage to enhance transparency in the investigation of King’s death.

title: Police fatally shoot person with knife at Weymouth park, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Weymouth police fatally shot a knife-wielding person in distress at Webb Memorial State Park, DA Michael Morrissey reports.
The shooting occurred around 4 p.m.; police provided medical aid, but the person died in the hospital.
The incident is under investigation by Massachusetts State Police; identities of those involved have not been released.
